## FIRST Compliance Assessment

- [x] **Fast** — All 16 generated tests run in < 30 ms total (measured: 0.03 s for 34 tests).
  No network calls, no disk I/O, no `time.sleep()`. The in-memory `EXPENSES` list
  is reset by the `reset_expenses` fixture in microseconds.

- [x] **Independent** — Every test class relies on the `autouse` `reset_expenses`
  fixture declared in `tests/conftest.py`. No test reads state written by another
  test. Tests can be run in any order with identical results.

- [x] **Repeatable** — No randomness, no time-of-day dependency, no environment
  variables, no network calls. Running the suite 10 times in a row produces the
  same 34 PASS / 0 FAIL result.

- [x] **Self-validating** — Every test contains at least one `assert` statement.
  Failure messages (via `pytest.approx` and f-string messages) describe exactly
  what was wrong and which bug ID the test guards against. No `print()` is used
  as a substitute for assertion.

- [x] **Timely** — All 16 tests cover only the three functions listed in
  `fix-summary.md` (`add_expense`, `get_total`, `get_expenses`). The generated
  file does not duplicate any test already present in `test_expense_tracker.py`.
  Bug IDs are referenced in test names and docstrings.
